task: Workflow to optimise settings for injection moulder (Engel 20t, unfilled PP, pin-gate-at-hub-centre polymer gear; goal = dimensional accuracy + no flaws/voids + best mechanical properties)
goal_notes: User satisfied with workflow and recalibration. No process data captured this session -- user deferred running shots. Workflow + pre-flight checklist + parameter ranking delivered. Next step: user runs Step 0 baseline and reports numbers.

human_feedback.back_pressure_challenge: User correctly challenged back pressure being framed as auxiliary. Agent recalibrated -- BP is top-tier for shear-sensitive PP with mechanical-property goals.

system_prompt: Claude Code default system prompt + global CLAUDE.md user rules (fix-the-system-not-the-instance, quality-over-effort, do-don't-ask, parallelism, subagent discipline, evaluator triggers).
skills: Forsy submission contract (fetched via WebFetch from https://formachine.forsy.ai/skill.md)
learning: For unfilled PP gears with pin-gate-at-hub geometry, the dominant defect mode is internal hub voids. Counter with: pack pressure in upper datasheet range, pack time >= gate-seal time, mould temp warm enough to keep hub fluid until pack arrives. When the optimisation goal includes mechanical properties, back pressure and melt temperature rise from auxiliary to top-tier importance because PP is shear-sensitive: excess recovery shear degrades molecular weight, which directly reduces tensile strength, toughness, creep resistance, and gear-tooth wear life.
